summary: Clients, orders, billing, support, products, announcements, and automation.
noOrders: No orders yet or API unreachable.
subject: Subject

card.ordersBody: Order queue with 6 digit order numbers.
card.emailsBody: SMTP settings, templates, and local outbox.
card.knowledgebaseBody: Public help articles and ticket reply inserts.
card.blogBody: Articles, SEO keywords, images, AI briefs.

cron.intro: The cron runner is triggered by an external HTTP request. Each call checks which jobs are due and runs them. Jobs are throttled — they only execute after their configured interval has passed.
cron.heartbeatHtml: Every trigger also writes a cron.started heartbeat and a cron.completed summary (with duration and per-job results), so you can confirm the cron is actually reaching the server. A wrong or missing secret is recorded as cron.unauthorized. See all of these under Settings → Logs in the Cron logs tab.
cron.activateHeading: How to Activate the Cron on Your Server
cron.activateIntroHtml: The cron is triggered by a POST (or GET) request to /api/v1/cron?token=YOUR_SECRET. Set up a system cron job on your server to call this endpoint on your preferred interval (e.g. hourly):
cron.replaceSecretHtml: Replace YOUR_SECRET with the Cron Secret set below (the optional CRON_SECRET server env var is also accepted) and use your own domain. Use the www host. A request to the bare domain (dezhost.com) is redirected to www.dezhost.com; a plain curl does not follow the redirect, so the request is dropped and the cron silently does nothing. You can also trigger it manually using the "Run Cron Now" button below.

desc.domainPrices: Syncs TLD pricing from Resell.biz into the domain price list.
desc.domainExpirations: Refreshes expiry dates for all registered domains.
desc.domainStatuses: Checks and updates domain registration statuses.
desc.billingMaintenance: Generates upcoming invoices, marks overdue invoices, and runs subscription renewals.
desc.invoiceReminders: Sends payment reminder emails to clients with invoices due within the configured days.
desc.ticketsClose: Auto-closes tickets that have been answered and have had no reply for the configured hours.
desc.mailboxes: Polls the support & sales IMAP mailboxes. Every fetched email is logged (with subject and matched client) and converted into a ticket; mail from an unknown sender creates a guest contact so nothing is lost. IMAP connection/login errors are recorded in the cron log.
desc.sitemap: Reports the live sitemap shape (URL counts) to the log. /sitemap.xml is served live by the website using the Site URL from Settings and the current published posts — no file is written, so it is always up to date.
desc.aiBlogPost: Generates an AI blog post when AI blogging is enabled and a Deepseek API key is set.

blogAdmin.title: Title
blogAdmin.excerptPlaceholder: One or two sentences — shown in blog listings
blogAdmin.aiRunning: Running — the cron job will generate articles on schedule.
blogAdmin.aiPaused: Paused — turn on to enable the cron-based schedule.
blogAdmin.topicsPoolHint: One topic per line. The AI picks one randomly and finds a fresh angle for each article.
blogAdmin.topicsPoolPlaceholder:
Web hosting security
Managed WordPress hosting
Email hosting tips
Domain name strategy
SSL certificates explained
Website speed optimization
blogAdmin.promptCustomizationHint: Leave blank to use sensible defaults. These instructions are appended to the Deepseek system prompt.
blogAdmin.titleGuidancePlaceholder: e.g. Keep titles under 65 chars, include main keyword
blogAdmin.keywordsGuidancePlaceholder: e.g. Target German SME market, include region names
blogAdmin.contentGuidancePlaceholder: e.g. Write for SME owners. Include practical examples. Use subheadings every 300 words. Friendly but professional tone.
blogAdmin.aiJobIntro: The AI blog job runs each time the main cron executes. It checks if enough time has passed since the last article and whether the daily limit is reached.
blogAdmin.validExceeds: {a} × {i}h = {t}h — exceeds 24 hours. Reduce articles or interval.
